gpt55_review_summary: Carson the 3rd reviewed with GPT-5.5, found merge-settings schema gaps, old-sidecar migration ambiguity, and missing auto-merge enforcement. The branch was updated to require all merge-setting keys, validate boolean values, enforce auto-merge, document/regress old-sidecar rejection, and Carson approved.
scope: GitHub governance and release-bundle sidecar validation only; benchmark execution, storage readers, engine adapters, and serving behavior are unchanged.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_github_governance.py tests/test_release_bundle.py::test_build_release_bundle_rejects_invalid_package_wheel_pr_evidence_or_github_governance tests/test_project_governance.py::test_github_docs_explain_branch_protection_application_and_plan_limit -q; poetry run pytest tests/test_github_governance.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q; poetry run pytest -q; poetry check; git diff --check; poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests
what_changed: Added GitHub repository merge settings to governance sidecars and strict release-bundle validation, including squash/rebase merge, GitHub auto-merge, and delete-branch-on-merge readiness.
why: The PR-driven release workflow requires automatic merge readiness and branch cleanup; strict release evidence should prove the repository settings support that process instead of relying on prose only.
